description: Critical test suites that must pass before every deploy to letsfg-website-preview.

guards: isoToMins extracts local airport time from ISO offset strings (not server UTC)
Dedup bucketing is consistent for +02:00 and no-offset timestamps of the same flight
selectDiverseTop slot assignment is consistent for offset vs bare timestamps
dedup key includes intermediate airport codes (LHR→FRA→JFK ≠ LHR→CDG→JFK)
Same routing from two connectors still collapses correctly
Offers with undefined timestamps do not crash the dedup path
rankOffers pre-filters duration > 48h (2880 min)
rankOffers pre-filters duration < 10 min
raw_offers_analyzed equals rawOffers.length (before any filter)
total_results equals post-filter normalized.length
The two values are not equal when some offers are filtered
total_duration_seconds is the canonical duration source
Epoch-diff duration is only used when both timestamps carry explicit tz info
Duration is 0 when no timing info is available
Cross-timezone short-haul (STN→BCN) produces correct minutes
Naive timestamp strings (no tz suffix) do not produce a duration from epoch diff
Rule 1: duration_too_short flagged for < 20 min
Rule 1: duration_too_long flagged for > 1440 min
Rule 2: arrival_before_departure flagged when arrival ≤ departure
Rule 3: timezone_drift flagged when |stored − epoch_diff| > 120 min
Rule 3: naive timestamps (no tz suffix) do not trigger timezone_drift
Rule 4: price_outlier flagged for price < 1 or > 20000
Rule 5: layover_anomaly flagged for segment layover > 720 min on sub-24h itinerary
validateOfferBatch correctly separates valid and suspect offers
validateOfferBatch handles empty batch without crashing
